intro: Eulophia zollingeri is a species of terrestrial orchid, commonly known as the 'Slipper Orchid', native to tropical Asia. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its various health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may help to treat conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections. The bioactive constituents of Eulophia zollingeri include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Eulophia zollingeri is typically prepared as a decoction or infusion, and is often used in herbal remedies, such as teas, tonics, and creams, to harness its medicinal benefits.
